title: UK Senior Applied AI Solution Engineer
jd_text:
About TomoroTomoro enables organisations to realise competitive advantage with the power of Generative AI. We work with large corporate clients to create meaningful AI strategies, build production-ready AI solutions and effectively integrate those solutions in their businesses.Our alliance with OpenAI and NVIDIA (among others) enables us to lead the industry in building valuable, scalable, enterprise-ready solutions for businesses. We're driven by applied R&D, prototyping and AI innovation. Our client teams are focused on tackling the most challenging aspects of applied AI in the enterprise sector directly with clients.About The RoleApplied AI solution engineers are expected to work in small teams of Tomoro and client engineers to design, build and deploy AI applications, such as agents built around Large Language Models. As your experience and expertise in the role grows, this may extend to leading these teams, owning solutions end-to-end and advising clients in this space. The typical applications we build use existing closed or open-source foundational models, potentially with some fine tuning. We generally do not need to train our own foundational models from scratch.RequirementsResponsibilitiesThe examples describe the types of responsibilities senior AI solution engineers at Tomoro will have.We do not expect every successful candidate to have experience in all of these areas. We encourage you to apply if the role excites you and you believe you can demonstrate a combination of the following capabilities.Visionary Leadership in Solution Development:As a Senior AI Solution Engineer, you'll be setting the vision for cutting-edge AI solutions and products. You'll own the delivery process from idea generation to deployment, ensuring that our AI-powered solutions are not only innovative but also aligned with our clients' strategic objectives.Advanced Analytical Problem-Solving:Your role involves a deep analytical approach to problem-solving. You will be responsible for breaking down complex challenges into manageable plans, making critical decisions that shape the direction of projects.Expertise in AI Application Development:With your strong background in designing, developing, testing, and deploying data and AI applications at an enterprise scale, you will provide technical leadership in building robust, efficient solutions that drive business transformation.Hands-on Development and Team Oversight:You will be comfortable with being both hands-on with coding and providing technical leadership and delivery oversight to teams of engineers. Your role balances the technical aspects of AI solution engineering with the leadership and guidance necessary to drive team success.Code Review and Mentorship:As a senior figure, you will manage and coach teams of engineers, fostering an environment of learning and growth, while providing critical feedback and ensuring the highest quality of code. Your guidance will be instrumental in developing the next generation of AI talent within Tomoro.Technical Stewardship across a Portfolio of Projects:You will work across multiple accounts or projects simultaneously, amplifying your impact through effective team leadership. Your ability to juggle various responsibilities will be key to the success of our projects.Contribution to Tomoro R&D product & asset development:Your role involves working within our R&D team contributing reusable assets to our core code repositories, thereby enhancing our collective knowledge base and technical capabilities.Senior Client Communication:Communicating with senior business and technical clients is a key part of your role. You will be responsible for explaining complex AI approaches, demonstrating solutions, and building strong, trusting relationships with our clients.Indicators you'll be a good fitBackground in software development:You have 5+ years of experience in developing software solutions for enterprise.Strong experience leading the design and development of production-grade solutions in enterprise, involving: Python applications and data processing Building microservices Large scale stateful distributed systems Large language modelsStrategic thinker and team leader: You are on the journey from being a strong individual contributor to managing and leading teams. You can demonstrate successful experience in both hands-on engineering and owning outcomes from inception to completion. You are comfortable setting out a vision for a solution, planning out tasks, managing teams and taking decisions. You are passionate and positive and can inspire and motivate teams to come together deliver in challengingStandard setter:You set high standards of technical excellence for yourself and your teams, providing guidance, identifying areas for improvement, and constantly striving towards better coding practices. circumstances.Effective communicator:You are comfortable explaining concepts and approaches to senior business leaders and both technical and non-technical audiences.Coach, mentor and educator:You have a history of supporting the professional development of engineers, creating a supportive environment which encourages learning and growth.You are committed to sharing knowledge and driving collective improvement across Tomoro and our clients' organisations."Multi-threaded" operator:At this stage of Tomoro's journey, we often need our senior team members to contribute to or oversee multiple projects at the same time. Your ability to balance your time and focus across a portfolio will be important for you to succeed.Innovator and thought leader:You proactively stay up to date with the latest developments in AI technology, connecting the dots and devising ways to apply new tools and techniques to real world problems faced by Tomoro and our clients. You contribute to the organisation's knowledge base and assets and are developing a reputation in the market for your insights and expertise.BenefitsPackageSalary range of £90,000 - £135,000  Holiday entitlement of 25 days + bank holidays Aviva Private medical insurance Medicash wellness cash plan (helps to cover your everyday healthcare needs) Life Policy Employee Assistance Programme (access to 24/7 helpline for in-the-moment support from qualified BACP counsellors) Company pension Access to exclusive discount platform Career CoachLocationHybrid working policy. May need to be flexible to travel to client offices as part of project work.

title: Habitat Technicians (derby)
jd_text:
Salary – £26K – £35K (full time) More than one vacancy available.Brindle & Green are currently seeking Habitat Technicians to join the contracting arm of our highly experienced multi-disciplinary practice.Our work is diverse and includes protected species fencing, badger sett creation and closures, habitat creation, habitat management, pond creation and more! You will liaise with inhouse ecologists, arboriculturists, landscapers and external contractors, to ensure work is delivered to requirements and within deadlines.Much of our work is on behalf a wide ranging developer client base and is often an extension of delivering the requirements of the technical outputs designed by our own ecology division. You will ideally have an estates management or agricultural background or similar with land management skills and qualifications.The role will include the use of machinery and plant, of which training will be provided prior to use. Tickets for excavator, saw, brushcutter etc are all desirable but aren’t essential for a willing and enthusiastic candidate. Your salary would be dependent on the level of skill/experience you join us with and there is plenty of opportunity for progression through the business for the right people.You will work as part of a small team and have access to a growing set of tools and equipment as well as the support and benefits that come from working within a growing consultancy.This role will involve working away from home. The frequency of this is carefully monitored depending on scope of works. Working Away Enhancements apply.Scope of works in the job role includes but is not limited to:Newt/reptile fencing.Other fencing (Post & rail, birdsmouth, subterranean badger fencing).Vegetation clearance & felling using brush cutters and chainsaws.Tree protection fencing.Habitat creation (Hibernacula, ponds).Grassland translocation.Planting/seeding/turfing.Badger sett closures.Artificial badger sett creation.FootpathsSwalesBat & bird box installation.Relevant Training/qualifications For This Role (desirable) IncludeLANTRA (or other work-based) diploma or certificate in outdoor conservation workConstruction safety CSCS card holderNPTC Chainsaw maintenance & crosscuttingNPTC Chainsaw – fellingNPTC Certificate of competence in brush cutting operationsNPTC Safe use of a brush woodchipperEmergency First Aid at WorkRTITB/CPCS/NPORS Tracked ExcavatorRTITB/CPCS/NPORS Dumper (5 ton)Salary based on experience and on a 40 hour per week contract. 28 days holiday entitlement including bank holidays, in addition to an extended period of paid leave over the Christmas. Company mobile phone provided and access to several departmental laptops as required. Uncapped training budget available to apply. Access to a fleet of work vehicles for business use.Our Vision serves as a guiding document that encapsulates our direction, structure, management and values, ensuring that every step we take is aligned with our mission to create value for our staff and clients. https://heyzine.com/flip-book/8639a1ead9.htmlIf you are interested in this vacancy, please send your CV and a covering letter by clicking Apply Here. (Please state vacancy reference: ‘Habitats Technician’)

title: Node.js Backend Engineer (TypeScript)
jd_text:
CloudWave Services requires a Node.js Backend Engineer with strong TypeScript expertise to join our Reading-based team. Your primary responsibility will be developing backend microservices that power our cloud platform, ensuring high availability and performance. You will write reusable and efficient code, design APIs, and integrate with various data stores. The ideal candidate has solid experience with Node.js, TypeScript, and asynchronous programming patterns. Familiarity with container orchestration (Kubernetes), cloud infrastructure (AWS or Azure), and CI/CD tools is essential. You'll work alongside frontend engineers and product owners in an agile environment focused on continuous improvement. This role offers opportunities to work on innovative cloud solutions in a supportive, technology-driven company.

title: Information Technology Support Engineer
jd_text:
IT Support EngineerSt Albans£35,000 - £45,000Our client who is a growing MSP require an experienced IT Support Engineer. No two days will be the same, as you will work across varied technical infrastructures. You will have the opportunity to develop your skills and play a pivotal part of a customer focused support team. Our client will offer you a supportive working environment. You will be responsible for resolving 1st – 3rd line tickets end to end. Responsibilities:Troubleshooting, installing, maintaining and supporting a wide range of IT systemsResolving 1st – 3rd line ticketsVisit client sites and working remotely via phone, email and remote accessWorking on projects, improvements and migrationsSupport windows server, RDS, AD, Hyper-V and Azure environmentsFully document tickets and update casesManage devices via RMM and IntuneSkills:Proven experience of working in a similar role for an MSPOutstanding customer service skillsStrong problem-solving skillsDriving license and own car (required for client visits)Good knowledge of networking fundamentalsStrong Microsoft 365 admin experienceWindows Server, AD, Intune, SecurityHyper V and RDS knowledgeExcellent communication skillsIdeally have azure depth, ConnectWise and Powershell experienceIf you are an experienced IT Support Engineer and looking for a new challenge, in a role which will offer you variety, real responsibility, direct access to decision-makers, and you have a true passion for technology, we’d love to hear from you. This role is office based 5 days a week.Due to number of applications, we will only be able to respond to candidates with the matching skills and experience.

title: Web Designer & Developer
jd_text:
Web Designer & Developer Location: Manchester (on-site) 37.5 hours a week, Mon - Fri. Salary: 30KDo you have a creative flair and a passion for designing and developing visually engaging, high-performing websites? Can you create clear, intuitive user journeys that not only look professional but also encourage visitors to take action and drive conversions?If you’re ready to take the next step in your career and become the go-to expert for website projects, UKExpertDigital is seeking a Website Designer & Developer to lead the delivery of high-performing websites that drive seamless, measurable results for our clients.We are part of the UKEGroup (UKExpertMedical, UKExpertDigital, Brain Injury Group, and Veterans Welfare Group). As a confident and inspiring expert in your field, you will work collaboratively with the wider UKED and marketing teams to optimise site functionality and performance, contributing to increased user engagement and enhanced search visibility for our websites.About UKExpertDigitalYou will be joining a skilled marketing team specialising in delivering online and offline marketing campaigns for its clients within the legal and medical industries supporting solicitors, charities and support groups. We pride ourselves in delivering the highest standards across all of our services to ensure the best results for our clients with a continuous improvement model at the heart of everything we deliver. We ensure our teams have clear progression paths and direct access to industry experts for development.Responsibilities  Serve as the lead spokesperson for web design & development, ensuring best practice standards are upheld across all brand and client websites.Create visual design concepts and create high-quality wireframes, mock-ups, and prototypes, collaborating with stakeholders to ensure visually engaging, fully responsive website layouts before the development stage.Develop and deliver live sites in line with stakeholder and client requirements ensuring testing is at the highest standardEnsure website content is quickly and easily accessible Serve as the lead spokesperson for web design & development, ensuring best-practice standards are upheld across all brand and client websites.Enhance user experience and oversee ongoing technical website maintenance for UKExpertDigital clientsConfigure and customise WordPress themes across client sites and support ongoing CMS updates.Produce detailed weekly reports outlining progress, insights, and continuous improvement initiatives.Continuously provide recommendations to improve UX and CRO across multiple represented brands.Apply and maintain SEO best practices throughout all web development activities.Communicate clearly with the marketing team and clients to interpret briefs, manage expectations, and set realistic deadlines.Prioritise and schedule content and development requests to ensure timely delivery and adherence to internal deadlines.Stay informed on emerging website and digital trends and technologies, sharing key updates with the team and implementing relevant advancements. Essential Skills  2 years’ experience in web design and website development.Proficient in WordPress, with the ability to manage and customise themes effectively.Skilled in design tools such as Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, Figma, or Sketch.Strong understanding of UI/UX best practices and principles.Solid knowledge of HTML, CSS and JavaScript.Demonstrated experience and understanding of Conversion Rate Optimisation (CRO).Good written English, with good grammar and attention to detail.Eager to learn, grow, and develop your specialist skills.Capable of working both independently and collaboratively within a team.Possess a strong creative flair and attention to aesthetic detail.Excellent communication skills and effective time-management abilities.Exceptional ability to prioritise tasks and manage workload efficiently. Desirable Skills   PHP experience for maintaining internal portals Good knowledge of SEO Hosting experience, server configuration & management

title: IT Field Engineer
jd_text:
Location: KetteringContract Type: PermanentHours: Full-time | MondayFriday, 9am5pm | Office-based in Kettering (with time onsite with clients)Salary: £25,000 per annumJoin an established Family Business Where You Truly Make a DifferenceAre you an enthusiastic, people-focused IT Engineer looking to build your career in a place where youre more than just a ticket number?Our client, a friendly and growing family-run IT business in Kettering, is looking for an IT Field Engineer to join their small, close-knit team. If you love solving problems, delivering brilliant customer service, and bringing your personality to work every day - this could be the perfect role for you.Why Youll Love This RoleA genuinely supportive, family environment where your ideas matterReal career growth as the business expandsA varied mix of office-based support and client-facing onsite workWork with a team that values personality, communication, and customer care just as much as tech skillsWhat Youll Be DoingThis is a hands-on, people-first role. title: API Developer (Node.js & TypeScript)
jd_text:
FinTech Innovations is recruiting an API Developer skilled in Node.js and TypeScript to enhance our financial services backend in Glasgow. You will develop and maintain APIs that handle high-volume transactions with reliability and security. The role includes designing scalable architecture, integrating with databases, and implementing security best practices. Candidates must have extensive experience with Node.js, TypeScript, and RESTful API development. Familiarity with OAuth, JWT, and encryption standards is preferred. Experience working within regulated industries and knowledge of compliance requirements is a plus. You will collaborate with DevOps and QA teams to ensure smooth deployment and monitoring. This is an exciting opportunity to build impactful financial technology in a fast-growing company.

title: TypeScript Node.js Developer
jd_text:
SecureNet Technologies is hiring a TypeScript Node.js Developer to build and maintain secure backend applications in Cambridge. The successful candidate will contribute to the design and development of APIs and microservices that handle sensitive data with high reliability. You must have a strong grasp of TypeScript and Node.js, with experience in writing clean, modular code. Responsibilities include API development, database schema design, and implementing authentication and authorization mechanisms. Familiarity with security best practices and GDPR compliance is crucial. Experience with cloud services (AWS preferred), containerization, and CI/CD pipelines is expected. You will work closely with security analysts and front-end developers to deliver seamless, secure solutions. This role is ideal for developers looking to advance their careers in a security-focused environment.
